title: juglans ailantifolia var. cordiformis
intro: Juglans ailantifolia var. cordiformis, also known as the Manchurian walnut, is a variety of the Chinese walnut tree. It is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, known for its edible nuts and medicinal properties. The bark of the Juglans ailantifolia var. cordiformis tree has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tree's nuts and leaves can be used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting immune function.
